user.title: Add HomieProxy instance
user.description: Create a new HTTP proxy endpoint. An initial access token will be generated automatically — you can manage tokens later via the integration's Configure dialog.

data.name: Endpoint name
data.restrict_out_cidrs: Custom outbound CIDRs (one per line, only used when 'Custom CIDR list' is selected)
data.restrict_in_cidrs: Inbound IP CIDR allow-list (one per line, blank = allow any)
data.requires_auth: Require Home Assistant authentication on top of the token

error.invalid_name: Name must be at least 2 characters
error.invalid_cidr: One or more CIDR entries are invalid
error.custom_requires_cidrs: Custom mode requires at least one CIDR
error.already_configured: An endpoint with this name already exists

abort.already_configured: An endpoint with this name already exists

init.title: Configure HomieProxy
init.description: Pick a section to edit. Changes apply immediately when you save each step.

rename.title: Rename endpoint
rename.description:
Current name: **{current_name}**

The name is the last segment of the endpoint URL — i.e. the part after `/api/homie_proxy/`. Changing it immediately updates the endpoint URL, so update any clients that reference the old name.

tokens.title: Manage access tokens
tokens.description: Currently {count} token(s). Edit the list (one per line) or pick an action below. Choosing an action other than 'Save changes' will append/replace tokens — pick 'Save changes' afterward to persist the result.

restrictions.title: Network restrictions
restrictions.description: Outbound mode controls which target URLs the proxy will call. 'Custom CIDR list' uses the outbound CIDRs below; the other modes ignore that field. Inbound CIDRs limit which client IPs may reach this endpoint (blank = any).

data.requires_auth: Require Home Assistant authentication on top of the token

info.title: Endpoint info (with help + examples)
info.description:
## This endpoint

| | |
|---|---|
| **Name** | `{name}` |
| **Path** | `{endpoint}` |
| **Tokens** | {token_count} configured |
| **Outbound mode** | `{restrict_out}` |
| **Outbound CIDRs** | {restrict_out_cidrs} |
| **Inbound CIDRs** | {restrict_in_cidrs} |
| **Requires HA auth** | {requires_auth} |
| **Timeout** | {timeout}s |

---

## URL pattern

```
http://HA_HOST:8123{endpoint}?token=YOUR_TOKEN&url=TARGET_URL
```

## Parameters

| Parameter | Description |
|---|---|
| `token` *(req)* | One of the configured access tokens |
| `url` *(req)* | Full target URL to proxy (e.g. `http://192.168.1.50/api`) |
| `timeout` | Per-request timeout override in seconds |
| `follow_redirects` | Set `true` to follow 3xx (default: pass back) |
| `skip_tls_checks` | Comma list: `all` · `self_signed` · `expired_cert` · `hostname_mismatch` · `cert_authority` |
| `cors_preflight` | `1` short-circuits `OPTIONS` with 204 |
| `request_header[X]` | Inject header *X* into the upstream request |
| `response_header[X]` | Attach header *X* to the response |

> Brackets must be percent-encoded in URLs: `[` → `%5B`, `]` → `%5D`. `URLSearchParams` handles this for you.

---

## Examples

**Basic GET (cURL):**
```
{curl_sample}
```

**POST with JSON body:**
```
{post_sample}
```

**Inject upstream auth + add CORS to response:**
```
{auth_sample}
```

**CORS preflight short-circuit (OPTIONS → 204):**
```
{preflight_sample}
```

**Skip TLS checks (self-signed cameras):**
```
{tls_sample}
```

**JavaScript (fetch):**
```javascript
{js_sample}
```

---

## Error codes

| Code | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `400` | `url` missing or malformed |
| `401` | Token missing or wrong |
| `403` | Client IP or target URL blocked by access control |
| `502` | Upstream unreachable (DNS / connection refused) |
| `504` | Upstream did not reply within the timeout |

---

**Debug endpoint** (requires HA login): [{debug_endpoint}]({debug_endpoint})

Click Submit to return to the menu.
